description: Immerse yourself in the vibrant culture and rich history of New Orleans, where jazz flows as freely as the Mississippi River. This 5-day adventure is designed for families who want to explore the city's unique blend of music, art, and culinary delights. From the lively streets of the French Quarter to the soulful sounds of live jazz, each day offers a new experience that captures the essence of this enchanting city. Get ready to indulge in local delicacies, discover hidden gems, and create unforgettable memories together!

activities.description: Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast of beignets and café au lait at the iconic Café du Monde. The powdered sugar-dusted pastries are a must-try!
Stroll through Jackson Square, a vibrant park surrounded by historic buildings. Enjoy the street performers and local artists showcasing their work, capturing the essence of New Orleans culture.
Savor authentic Creole cuisine at The Gumbo Shop, known for its rich gumbo and jambalaya. This cozy restaurant is perfect for a leisurely lunch in the heart of the French Quarter.
Explore the stunning St. Louis Cathedral, the oldest continuously active Roman Catholic cathedral in the United States. Its beautiful architecture and serene atmosphere make it a peaceful stop.
Join a guided tour focusing on the rich history of jazz in New Orleans. Learn about legendary musicians and the evolution of jazz while visiting significant locations in the city.
Head to local shops in the French Quarter to find unique jazz-themed merchandise, from vinyl records to T-shirts. It's a great way to take a piece of New Orleans home with you!
Enjoy a casual dinner at Coop's Place, famous for its fried chicken and seafood gumbo. The laid-back atmosphere and delicious food make it a local favorite.
End your day with an unforgettable live jazz performance at Preservation Hall. This historic venue is dedicated to preserving New Orleans jazz and offers an intimate setting for music lovers.

Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast at the iconic Café du Monde. Enjoy their famous beignets dusted with powdered sugar and a cup of rich café au lait, all while soaking in the vibrant atmosphere of the French Quarter.

Dive into the heart of New Orleans jazz at Preservation Hall. This historic venue offers intimate concerts featuring some of the best local musicians. Be sure to check the schedule for performances and arrive early to secure your spot!

Savor a delicious lunch at Coop's Place, known for its authentic Cajun and Creole dishes. Try their famous jambalaya or gumbo for a true taste of New Orleans. The casual atmosphere makes it a perfect spot to relax and refuel.

Stroll down Frenchmen Street, a hub for live music and local art. Browse through unique shops and galleries, and keep an eye out for street performers. This area is perfect for picking up some jazz-themed merchandise and souvenirs.

Take a leisurely walk through Louis Armstrong Park, a beautiful green space dedicated to the legendary jazz musician. Enjoy the sculptures and fountains, and if you're lucky, you might catch a live performance in the park.

Indulge in a hearty dinner at The Gumbo Shop, where you can enjoy a variety of gumbo dishes and other local specialties. The cozy atmosphere and friendly service make it a great place to unwind after a day of exploring.

End your day with a live jazz concert at Snug Harbor, one of the best venues in the city. Enjoy the smooth sounds of talented musicians while sipping on a cocktail. It's the perfect way to immerse yourself in the local jazz scene.
Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast of beignets and café au lait at the iconic Café du Monde. The powdered sugar-dusted pastries are a must-try!
Stroll through the picturesque Garden District, known for its stunning antebellum mansions and lush gardens. Don't forget to take photos of the beautiful architecture and vibrant flora.
Enjoy a delightful lunch at Commander's Palace, a renowned restaurant famous for its Creole cuisine. The turtle soup and bread pudding soufflé are highly recommended!
Explore the historic Lafayette Cemetery No. 1, known for its above-ground tombs and rich history. It's a fascinating glimpse into New Orleans' unique burial traditions.
Head to Magazine Street for some shopping! This area is filled with local boutiques, art galleries, and shops where you can find unique jazz-related merchandise and souvenirs.
Experience the heart of New Orleans jazz at Preservation Hall. Enjoy an intimate performance by local musicians in this historic venue, a true gem for jazz lovers.
End your day with a casual dinner at Coop's Place, known for its delicious Cajun and Creole dishes. The jambalaya and fried chicken are local favorites!

Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast at the iconic Café du Monde. Enjoy their famous beignets dusted with powdered sugar and a cup of rich café au lait, all while soaking in the lively atmosphere of the French Quarter.

activities.activity: Swamp Tour at Jean Lafitte National Historical Park
activities.description: Embark on an exciting swamp tour at Jean Lafitte National Historical Park. Glide through the bayous and marshes, where you might spot alligators, turtles, and a variety of birds. This is a fantastic way to experience the unique ecosystem of Louisiana.
After your swamp adventure, head to The Gumbo Shop for a delicious lunch. Savor authentic Creole dishes, including their famous gumbo and jambalaya, in a cozy setting that reflects the local culture.

Explore the vibrant French Market, where you can find local crafts, fresh produce, and unique souvenirs. This is the perfect spot to pick up some jazz-related merchandise and other local goodies to take home.

Dive into the rich history of jazz at the New Orleans Jazz Museum. Located in the historic Old U.S. Mint, the museum showcases the evolution of jazz music and its cultural significance in New Orleans.
End your day with a hearty dinner at Coop's Place, known for its casual vibe and delicious Cajun cuisine. Try their famous rabbit and sausage jambalaya for a true taste of New Orleans.

Cap off your evening with a live jazz performance at Preservation Hall. This legendary venue is dedicated to preserving the art of New Orleans jazz, offering an intimate setting to enjoy some of the best local musicians.

departure.place: Louis Armstrong New Orleans International Airport

transit.memo: Direct flight back to Washington D.C.

activities.description: Start your day with a classic New Orleans breakfast at the iconic Café du Monde. Enjoy their famous beignets dusted with powdered sugar and a café au lait to fuel your day of exploration.

activities.activity: Visit the New Orleans Jazz Museum
activities.description: Dive into the rich history of jazz at the New Orleans Jazz Museum. Located in the historic Old U.S. Mint, the museum showcases exhibits on jazz legends and the evolution of this vibrant music genre.
Savor some authentic Cajun cuisine at Coop's Place, known for its laid-back atmosphere and delicious dishes like jambalaya and fried chicken. A perfect spot to recharge before more sightseeing.
Stroll down Frenchmen Street, a hub for live music and local art. Check out the various shops for unique jazz memorabilia and local crafts, perfect for souvenirs and gifts.
Take a leisurely walk through Louis Armstrong Park, dedicated to the legendary jazz musician. Enjoy the beautiful landscapes and perhaps catch some live music in the park.
End your New Orleans adventure with dinner at The Gumbo Shop, where you can indulge in a bowl of their famous gumbo or étouffée, surrounded by the lively atmosphere of the French Quarter.
